channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Coping Strategies and Experiences of Patients With Crohn's Disease During the COVID-19 Epidemic: A Descriptive Phenomenology Study.
item.description: Nursing open - To describe the flexible coping strategies and measures made by Crohn's disease patients during the COVID-19 epidemic to determine their experiences with COVID-19 and epidemic management. A descrip...

item.title: Association Between Web-Based Patient Portal Messaging and Disease Activity in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Retrospective Pilot Cohort Study.
item.description: JMIR human factors - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) requires ongoing monitoring and effective communication between patients and health care providers. Web-based patient portals may enhance engagement, but their asso...

item.title: The protein tyrosine phosphatase CD45 promotes PMN transepithelial migration, antimicrobial function and colonic mucosal repair.
item.description: JCI insight - Polymorphonuclear neutrophils (PMNs) serve as frontline defenders against injury and infection, eliminating pathogens and initiating mucosal tissue repair. However, excessive PMN transepithelial mi...

item.title: Ameliorative effect of MPAF on DSS-induced ulcerative colitis mice.
item.description: Molecular biology reports - Ulcerative Colitis (UC) is a common chronic inflammatory intestinal disease. The nut protein peptides are not only easily digested and absorbed, but also possess various physiological functions. Th...

item.title: Multi-Component Functionalized Bifidobacterium Longum Hydrogel for Multi-Target Integrated Therapy of Colitis-Associated Anxiety and Depression.
item.description: Advanced science (Weinheim, Baden-Wurttemberg, Germany) - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs) are frequently accompanied by anxiety and depression, largely driven by perturbed gut-brain axis signaling. However, current oral therapies remain constrained by ...

item.title: Membranous nephropathy secondary to very early-onset inflammatory bowel disease in a 3-year-old boy: case report.
item.description: CEN case reports - Case report - Membranous nephropathy (MN) is uncommon in children overall, but secondary MN is relatively common in younger children. Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) can be complicated by kidney disease, but IB...

item.title: Associations of dietary habits and meal regularity with gastrointestinal symptoms, depression, and quality of life in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a cross-sectional study.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) is a chronic and recurrent gastrointestinal disorder that adversely affects patients' quality of life. The influence of daily dietary behaviors and meal regularity ...

item.title: Mapping the neuroimaging landscape of inflammatory bowel disease: a bibliometric analysis and systematic scoping review.
item.description: Frontiers in neuroscience - Systematic review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is recognized as a prototypical disorder of brain-gut interaction. Although neuroimaging research in this field has advanced rapidly in recent years, the findings r...
